pi_items.source: Gray (Gy) is the SI unit for absorbed radiation dose in medical physics. Confirmed via Wikipedia and NRC documentation. Appropriate for radiochromic film dosimetry calibration.

Analyzed actual Blobs sample image (256x254 pixels, 8-bit grayscale, 21 KB) downloaded from https://samples.fiji.sc/. Applied calibration mapping: Pixel 0 → 5.0 Gy, Pixel 255 → 0.0 Gy. Darkest pixel in image is 8, which maps to 4.8431 Gy.
Same Blobs image analysis. Maximum possible dose (pixel 0) would be exactly 5.0 Gy. Actual maximum in image is 4.8431 Gy, which is within the metadata range of 3.0-6.0.
Statistical analysis of Blobs image: mean pixel value is 103.27, which calibrates to 2.9751 Gy. This is above the minimum of 0.5 Gy.
Statistical analysis of Blobs image: mean calibrated dose is 2.9751 Gy, which exceeds the maximum expected value of 2.5 Gy by 19%.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the ImageJ/Fiji Blobs (25K) sample image, a standard 256x254 pixel 8-bit grayscale test image (21 KB) available at https://samples.fiji.sc/blobs.png. The image is real, not synthetic. After applying the specified intensity calibration (Pixel 0 → 5.0 Gy, Pixel 255 → 0.0 Gy), actual measured statistics are: maximum dose 4.8431 Gy (within metadata range 3.0-6.0), mean dose 2.9751 Gy (exceeds metadata maximum of 2.5), minimum dose 0.1373 Gy. The unit Gy (Gray) is the correct SI unit for absorbed radiation dose in medical physics. One metadata claim is contradicted: max_expected_mean_dose should be approximately 3.0 instead of 2.5.
